What is an ERP system?

📘 Definition of ERP System

Definition:
Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system is a software solution that integrates various business functions into a single platform to manage and automate processes.

Key Activities:
• Centralized data management
• Process automation
• Real-time reporting

Related Modules:
• Financial Management
• Human Resources
• Supply Chain Management

Quick Difference: ERP systems differ from traditional accounting software in their ability to integrate multiple business functions, providing a more holistic view of the organization.

What does ERP stand for?

📘 Definition of ERP

Definition: Enterprise Resource Planning

Key Activities:
• Implementation and configuration
• Data integration and management
• Process optimization

Related Modules:
• Financial Management
• Human Capital Management
• Supply Chain Management

Quick Difference (if comparing concepts):
ERP is a comprehensive software solution that integrates various business functions, whereas CRM (Customer Relationship Management) focuses on customer interactions and relationships.

What is the difference between ERP and CRM?

📘 Definition: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) vs Customer Relationship Management (CRM)

• Analyzing business processes
• Identifying software features

What is a Purchase Requisition (PR)?

📘 Definition
A Purchase Requisition (PR) is a formal request from an employee or department to purchase goods or services for the organization.

Key Activities:
• Initiate PR
• Approve PR
• Process Payment

Related Modules:
• Procurement Module
• Accounts Payable Module

Quick Difference: A PR is similar to a Purchase Order, but it's a preliminary request that needs approval before the actual order is placed.

response.summary:
I'm EDIP AI, your ERP Business Assistant.

What is a Purchase Order (PO)?

📘 Definition
A Purchase Order (PO) is an official document sent by a buyer to a supplier requesting goods or services in exchange for payment.

Key Activities:
• Issuance of PO
• Receiving and processing POs
• Tracking and managing PO status

Related Modules:
• Procurement Module
• Inventory Management Module

Quick Difference: A Purchase Order is similar to an Invoice, but it's used by the buyer to request goods or services from a supplier.

results.question: how many sales orders are in the system?

response.sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) AS total_sales_orders FROM Sales_Masters.SalesOrder_Header AS T1
SELECT T1.id, T1.name, T1.email, T1.phone FROM masters.customers AS T1
SELECT T1.id, T1.name, T1.email, T1.phone FROM masters.suppliers AS T1

results.question: how many items are active in the system?

response.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM masters.items WHERE active = 1;

results.question: what is the highest value invoice?

response.sql: SELECT T1.invoice_id, T1.customer_name, T1.total FROM Sales_Masters.Invoice_Header AS T1 ORDER BY T1.total DESC LIMIT 10
